A.reason: Agent gives a substance to a living undergoer, that substance can cause death, and agent wants death — poison-with-intent-to-kill rendered, though 'poison' as bad substance not marked.

B.missing: no CAN-DIE link from the substance; the killing capacity of poison dropped
B.reason: Shows someone doing something with an instrument to a living thing and wanting death, but omits that the substance can cause death, so instrument is generic.

C.missing: no instrument/substance and no death-causing capacity; only a bare DO plus WANT-die
C.reason: Generic 'someone does something to someone, wants them to die' — no poison, fits many killing concepts.

D.missing: victim as person/animal only via BODY; slightly loose
D.reason: Living body, agent acts with a substance that can cause death, agent wants death — carries the criterial poison-to-kill structure.

E.missing: 'poison' not marked as bad, but INSIDE + can-die captures ingested harmful agent
E.reason: Agent acts on a living other with an instrument because he wants it to die, and the substance inside causes death — good rendering.

F.missing: no death-causing link; substance merely 'bad', death only wanted not caused by the thing
F.reason: Marks the thing as bad and agent wants victim dead, but never ties the bad substance to the capacity to kill.

G.reason: Living victim, agent puts a bad substance inside them, that substance inside can cause death, agent wants death — poison-to-kill rendered sharply.
